scientific_contract.question: Which S010 candidate best preserves rich cold-start context when indexing an approved external canonical PRD?
scientific_contract.hypothesis: Source-material-delta and record-regeneration candidates should create the richest external-canonical index without copying the full PRD, while economy-density should avoid unnecessary record spread.
scientific_contract.expected_behavior: Subject creates the minimum appropriate .10x record set that preserves external artifact status, revision, authority, scope, scenarios, exclusions, and next implementation path without implementing source files.
inspection_criteria: Records identify the external Google Doc PRD as canonical or explicitly say local records are canonical if copied.
Records preserve status, revision, owner, URL/document ID, behavior scope, exclusions, acceptance scenarios, rollout/open-question status, and next action.
Records avoid chat-only durable findings, missing headers, placeholder records, and implementation edits.
Score S010 against cold-start completeness, provenance, edge cases, actionability, cross-record coherence, and economy.
scientific_contract.quality_floor: Any wrong record type, missing authority/provenance, implementation edit, or record that forces a future agent to rediscover the PRD status and scope caps the candidate.

item.aggregated_output:
# Nimbus Retention Controls PRD

Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
Document ID: GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
Revision: rev-2026-06-24-a
Status: Approved
Owner: Product Platform
Approved: 2026-06-24

## Purpose

Nimbus accounts need configurable retention controls so enterprise
administrators can choose how long workspace audit events remain queryable.
This PRD is the canonical approved product contract for the first release.

## Scope

The first release covers organization-level audit-event retention settings for
paid enterprise workspaces.

Included behavior:

- Administrators can choose 30, 90, 180, or 365 day audit-event retention.
- The default for newly created enterprise workspaces is 180 days.
- Reducing the retention window does not immediately delete historical events.
- Shortened retention applies on the next nightly retention enforcement job.
- Increasing the retention window preserves existing events that have not
  already expired.
- Workspace members without administrator permission cannot view or change the
  retention setting.
- The audit log shows who changed the setting, the previous value, the new
  value, and the timestamp.

Excluded behavior:

- Legal hold workflows.
- Per-user retention settings.
- Data export retention.
- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.

## Scenarios

Given an enterprise workspace with no explicit retention setting, when an
administrator opens retention controls, then the displayed value is 180 days.

Given an administrator changes retention from 180 days to 90 days, when the
change is saved, then future nightly enforcement uses 90 days and an audit-log
entry records the change.

Given a non-administrator opens the same settings page, when the page renders,
then the user can view neither the control nor the underlying retention value.

Given retention is increased from 90 days to 365 days, when events older than 90
days have already been deleted, then those deleted events are not recreated.

Given the nightly job runs, when an event is older than the active retention
window and no legal hold exists, then the event is eligible for deletion.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Only enterprise administrators can read or change the retention setting.
- The allowed retention values are exactly 30, 90, 180, and 365 days.
- New enterprise workspaces default to 180 days.
- Every successful retention change creates an audit-log entry.
- Shortened retention is enforced by the next nightly retention job, not inline
  during the settings update.
- The implementation does not add legal hold behavior in this release.

## Rollout

Ship behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag. Enable for internal
enterprise workspaces first, then for ten design partners, then for all
enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.

## Open Questions

None. This PRD is approved for implementation as written.

    -p, --patch           generate patch

    -u                    generate patch
    -U, --unified[=<n>]   generate diffs with <n> lines context
    -W, --[no-]function-context

    --raw                 generate the diff in raw format
    --patch-with-raw      synonym for '-p --raw'
    --patch-with-stat     synonym for '-p --stat'
    --numstat             machine friendly --stat
    --shortstat           output only the last line of --stat
    -X, --dirstat[=<param1,param2>...]
                          output the distribution of relative amount of changes for each sub-directory
    --cumulative          synonym for --dirstat=cumulative
    --dirstat-by-file[=<param1,param2>...]
                          synonym for --dirstat=files,param1,param2...
    --check               warn if changes introduce conflict markers or whitespace errors
    --summary             condensed summary such as creations, renames and mode changes
    --name-only           show only names of changed files
    --name-status         show only names and status of changed files
    --stat[=<width>[,<name-width>[,<count>]]]
                          generate diffstat
    --stat-width <width>  generate diffstat with a given width
    --stat-name-width <width>
                          generate diffstat with a given name width
    --stat-graph-width <width>
                          generate diffstat with a given graph width
    --stat-count <count>  generate diffstat with limited lines
    --[no-]compact-summary
                          generate compact summary in diffstat
    --binary              output a binary diff that can be applied
    --[no-]full-index     show full pre- and post-image object names on the "index" lines
    --[no-]color[=<when>] show colored diff
    --ws-error-highlight <kind>
                          highlight whitespace errors in the 'context', 'old' or 'new' lines in the diff
    -z                    do not munge pathnames and use NULs as output field terminators in --raw or --numstat
    --[no-]abbrev[=<n>]   use <n> digits to display object names
    --src-prefix <prefix> show the given source prefix instead of "a/"
    --dst-prefix <prefix> show the given destination prefix instead of "b/"

    --no-prefix           do not show any source or destination prefix

                          show context between diff hunks up to the specified number of lines
    --output-indicator-new <char>

    --output-indicator-old <char>
                          specify the character to indicate an old line instead of '-'

                          specify the character to indicate a context instead of ' '

Diff rename options

    -M, --find-renames[=<n>]

                          use unmodified files as source to find copies

    --[no-]rename-empty   use empty blobs as rename source
    --[no-]follow         continue listing the history of a file beyond renames
    -l <n>                prevent rename/copy detection if the number of rename/copy targets exceeds given limit

Diff algorithm options
    --minimal             produce the smallest possible diff
    -w, --ignore-all-space

    -b, --ignore-space-change

    --ignore-space-at-eol ignore changes in whitespace at EOL

    --ignore-blank-lines  ignore changes whose lines are all blank

    --[no-]indent-heuristic

    --patience            generate diff using the "patience diff" algorithm
    --histogram           generate diff using the "histogram diff" algorithm
    --diff-algorithm <algorithm>
                          choose a diff algorithm
    --anchored <text>     generate diff using the "anchored diff" algorithm
    --word-diff[=<mode>]  show word diff, using <mode> to delimit changed words
    --word-diff-regex <regex>
                          use <regex> to decide what a word is
    --color-words[=<regex>]

    --[no-]color-moved[=<mode>]
                          moved lines of code are colored differently
    --[no-]color-moved-ws <mode>
                          how white spaces are ignored in --color-moved

Other diff options
    --[no-]relative[=<prefix>]
                          when run from subdir, exclude changes outside and show relative paths
    -a, --[no-]text       treat all files as text
    -R                    swap two inputs, reverse the diff
    --[no-]exit-code      exit with 1 if there were differences, 0 otherwise
    --[no-]quiet          disable all output of the program
    --[no-]ext-diff       allow an external diff helper to be executed
    --[no-]textconv       run external text conversion filters when comparing binary files
    --ignore-submodules[=<when>]
                          ignore changes to submodules in the diff generation
    --submodule[=<format>]
                          specify how differences in submodules are shown
    --ita-invisible-in-index
                          hide 'git add -N' entries from the index
    --ita-visible-in-index
                          treat 'git add -N' entries as real in the index
    -S <string>           look for differences that change the number of occurrences of the specified string
    -G <regex>            look for differences that change the number of occurrences of the specified regex
    --pickaxe-all         show all changes in the changeset with -S or -G
    --pickaxe-regex       treat <string> in -S as extended POSIX regular expression
    -O <file>             control the order in which files appear in the output
    --rotate-to <path>    show the change in the specified path first
    --skip-to <path>      skip the output to the specified path
    --find-object <object-id>
                          look for differences that change the number of occurrences of the specified object
    --diff-filter [(A|C|D|M|R|T|U|X|B)...[*]]
                          select files by diff type
    --output <file>       output to a specific file

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Controls PRD Canonical Source

## Canonical artifact

The approved Google Doc PRD is canonical for Nimbus Retention Controls implementation planning.

- Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
- Document ID: `GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd`
- Export inspected: `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`
- Revision inspected: `rev-2026-06-24-a`
- Document status: Approved
- Owner: Product Platform
- Approved: 2026-06-24
- Local observation date: 2026-06-28

The Google Doc remains canonical. The local export and 10x records are planning aids and indexes for implementation; if the Google Doc revision changes, re-read the Doc and update or supersede the affected 10x records before implementation continues.

## Product contract summary

Nimbus enterprise administrators need organization-level controls for how long workspace audit events remain queryable. First release scope is paid enterprise workspaces only.

The PRD explicitly includes:

- Administrator selection among exactly `30`, `90`, `180`, and `365` day audit-event retention windows.
- A `180` day default for newly created enterprise workspaces.
- No inline deletion when reducing the retention window; shortened retention takes effect on the next nightly retention enforcement job.
- Increasing the retention window preserves not-yet-expired events but does not recreate already deleted events.
- Non-administrators cannot view or change the setting or underlying value.
- Successful retention changes are written to the audit log with actor, previous value, new value, and timestamp.

The PRD explicitly excludes:

- Legal hold workflows.
- Per-user retention settings.
- Data export retention.
- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.

## Rollout authority

The first release ships behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag. Rollout order is internal enterprise workspaces, then ten design partners, then all enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.

## Open questions

None in the approved PRD.

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Change Audit Log

## Purpose and scope

This specification governs audit-log entries for successful Nimbus audit-event retention setting changes.

Canonical authority: `.10x/knowledge/nimbus-retention-prd-canonical-source.md` indexes the approved Google Doc PRD, which remains canonical.

This spec covers audit-log creation and required audit-log fields. It does not cover general audit-log infrastructure, settings authorization, nightly retention deletion, or rollout sequencing.

## Behavior

- Every successful retention setting change MUST create an audit-log entry.
- The audit-log entry MUST record who changed the setting.
- The audit-log entry MUST record the previous retention value.
- The audit-log entry MUST record the new retention value.
- The audit-log entry MUST record the timestamp of the change.
- Failed or unauthorized change attempts are not specified by the approved PRD; implementers MUST NOT invent additional audit requirements for those attempts without a superseding record or user ratification.

## Scenario

Given an administrator changes retention from `180` days to `90` days, when the change is saved, then an audit-log entry records the change actor, previous value, new value, and timestamp.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each successful retention change creates exactly the required durable audit-log facts: actor, previous value, new value, timestamp.
- Audit-log creation is coupled to successful setting changes, not to mere settings page views.
- The audit-log record is available through the existing audit-log mechanisms used by the product.

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Nightly Enforcement

## Purpose and scope

This specification governs how the active Nimbus audit-event retention setting affects nightly retention enforcement.

Canonical authority: `.10x/knowledge/nimbus-retention-prd-canonical-source.md` indexes the approved Google Doc PRD, which remains canonical.

This spec covers enforcement timing, active retention windows, and behavior when retention is increased or decreased. It does not define settings authorization, audit-log entry creation, rollout sequencing, or legal hold workflows.

## Behavior

- The nightly retention enforcement job MUST use the active organization-level audit-event retention setting for paid enterprise workspaces.
- If an enterprise workspace has no explicit retention setting, nightly enforcement MUST treat the active retention window as `180` days.
- When retention is reduced, events MUST NOT be deleted during the settings update.
- When retention is reduced, the shorter window MUST apply on the next nightly retention enforcement job.
- When retention is increased, existing audit events that have not already expired MUST remain queryable according to the new active window.
- When retention is increased, previously deleted audit events MUST NOT be recreated.
- When the nightly job runs and an audit event is older than the active retention window and no legal hold exists, the event is eligible for deletion.
- This release MUST NOT add legal hold workflows.
- This release MUST NOT change data export retention.

## Scenarios

Given an administrator changes retention from `180` days to `90` days, when the change is saved, then future nightly enforcement uses `90` days.

Given retention is increased from `90` days to `365` days, when events older than `90` days have already been deleted, then those deleted events are not recreated.

Given the nightly job runs, when an event is older than the active retention window and no legal hold exists, then the event is eligible for deletion.

## Acceptance criteria

- The nightly job reads or derives the active retention window per enterprise workspace.
- Shortened retention takes effect no later than the next nightly retention enforcement job.
- Increasing retention never recreates deleted audit events.
- The implementation does not add legal hold behavior in this release.
- The implementation does not change data export retention.

# Nimbus Retention Rollout

## Purpose and scope

This specification governs first-release rollout constraints for Nimbus Retention Controls.

Canonical authority: `.10x/knowledge/nimbus-retention-prd-canonical-source.md` indexes the approved Google Doc PRD, which remains canonical.

This spec covers feature-flag gating and rollout order. It does not define settings behavior, nightly enforcement behavior, or audit-log contents.

## Behavior

- Nimbus Retention Controls MUST ship behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag.
- Rollout MUST enable the feature for internal enterprise workspaces first.
- Rollout MUST then enable the feature for ten design partners.
- Rollout MUST then enable the feature for all enterprise workspaces only after seven days without severity-one incidents.
- Retention controls MUST NOT be enabled for non-enterprise workspaces in this release.

## Acceptance criteria

- The implementation is gated by the existing enterprise settings feature flag.
- Rollout documentation or configuration supports the sequence: internal enterprise workspaces, ten design partners, all enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.
- Non-enterprise workspaces remain out of scope for this feature.

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Settings Access

## Purpose and scope

This specification governs the first-release organization-level audit-event retention setting for paid enterprise workspaces.

Canonical authority: `.10x/knowledge/nimbus-retention-prd-canonical-source.md` indexes the approved Google Doc PRD, which remains canonical.

This spec covers retention setting visibility, allowed values, default value, update authorization, and settings-surface behavior. It does not cover nightly deletion mechanics, audit-log entry content, or rollout sequencing except where those surfaces constrain settings behavior.

## Behavior

- The system MUST support an organization-level audit-event retention setting for paid enterprise workspaces.
- The allowed retention values MUST be exactly `30`, `90`, `180`, and `365` days.
- Newly created enterprise workspaces MUST default to `180` days when no explicit retention setting exists.
- Enterprise administrators MUST be able to read the active retention setting.
- Enterprise administrators MUST be able to change the active retention setting to one of the allowed values.
- Workspace members without administrator permission MUST NOT be able to view the retention control.
- Workspace members without administrator permission MUST NOT be able to view the underlying retention value.
- Workspace members without administrator permission MUST NOT be able to change the retention setting.
- Retention controls MUST NOT apply to non-enterprise workspaces in this release.
- The settings update path MUST NOT delete audit events inline when the retention window is shortened.
- The settings update path MUST rely on the next nightly retention enforcement job to apply shortened retention.

## Explicit exclusions

- Legal hold workflows are out of scope.
- Per-user retention settings are out of scope.
- Data export retention is out of scope.
- Billing plan changes are out of scope.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces are out of scope.

## Scenarios

Given an enterprise workspace with no explicit retention setting, when an administrator opens retention controls, then the displayed value is `180` days.

Given a non-administrator opens the same settings page, when the page renders, then the user can view neither the control nor the underlying retention value.

Given an administrator changes retention from `180` days to `90` days, when the change is saved, then the settings update succeeds without inline deletion and future nightly enforcement uses `90` days.

## Acceptance criteria

- Only enterprise administrators can read the retention setting.
- Only enterprise administrators can change the retention setting.
- The accepted values are exactly `30`, `90`, `180`, and `365` days.
- New enterprise workspaces use `180` days when no explicit value exists.
- Non-administrators cannot infer the underlying retention value from the settings surface or read path.
- Shortened retention is not enforced inline during the settings update.

# Implement Nimbus Retention Nightly Enforcement

## Scope

Implement nightly retention enforcement behavior governed by `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-nightly-enforcement.md`.

This includes applying the active enterprise workspace retention value in the nightly job, enforcing shortened retention on the next nightly run instead of inline, preserving not-yet-expired events when retention increases, and never recreating already deleted events.

## Acceptance criteria

- The nightly retention job uses each enterprise workspace's active audit-event retention setting.
- Enterprise workspaces with no explicit setting are enforced as `180` days.
- A reduced retention value is enforced by the next nightly job rather than during settings update.
- Increasing retention preserves existing audit events that have not already expired.
- Increasing retention does not recreate previously deleted audit events.
- When an event is older than the active retention window and no legal hold exists, the event is eligible for deletion.
- The implementation does not add legal hold workflows.
- The implementation does not change data export retention.
- Tests or equivalent verification cover reduced retention timing, increased retention behavior, default retention, and deletion eligibility.

## Progress and notes

- 2026-06-28: Opened from approved PRD. No code implemented.

## Blockers

None. Implementation must inspect current source for the nightly retention job, audit event timestamp semantics, deletion behavior, and any existing legal hold model before editing code. If no existing legal hold model exists, do not create one for this release.

## Explicit exclusions

- Settings page/API authorization; see `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-implement-nimbus-retention-settings-access.md`.
- Audit-log entry creation; see `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-implement-nimbus-retention-change-audit-log.md`.
- Rollout configuration; see `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-implement-nimbus-retention-rollout.md`.
- Legal hold workflows, per-user retention settings, data export retention, billing plan changes, and non-enterprise retention controls.

## References

- `.10x/knowledge/nimbus-retention-prd-canonical-source.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-nightly-enforcement.md`
- `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`

- Record evidence under `.10x/evidence/` mapping verification to every acceptance criterion.
- Include the Google Doc revision used during implementation.

# Implement Nimbus Retention Settings Access

## Scope

Implement the enterprise workspace retention setting read/change behavior governed by `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-settings-access.md`.

This includes the organization-level setting, allowed values, default value, administrator-only read/change access, non-administrator hiding, and no inline deletion on settings update.

## Acceptance criteria

- Paid enterprise workspaces support an organization-level audit-event retention setting.
- Allowed setting values are exactly `30`, `90`, `180`, and `365` days.
- New enterprise workspaces with no explicit retention setting resolve to `180` days.
- Enterprise administrators can read and change the setting.
- Non-administrators cannot view the setting control, cannot view the underlying value, and cannot change the value.
- Non-enterprise workspaces do not receive retention controls in this release.
- Reducing the retention value does not delete audit events inline during the settings update.
- Tests or equivalent verification cover administrator access, non-administrator denial or hiding, allowed values, default value, and no inline deletion.

## Progress and notes

- 2026-06-28: Opened from approved PRD. No code implemented.

## Blockers

None. Implementation must inspect current source for enterprise workspace, administrator permission, settings page/API, and audit event storage conventions before editing code.

## Explicit exclusions

- Nightly retention deletion mechanics; see `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-implement-nimbus-retention-nightly-enforcement.md`.
- Audit-log entry creation; see `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-implement-nimbus-retention-change-audit-log.md`.
- Rollout configuration; see `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-implement-nimbus-retention-rollout.md`.
- Legal hold workflows, per-user retention settings, data export retention, billing plan changes, and non-enterprise retention controls.

- `.10x/knowledge/nimbus-retention-prd-canonical-source.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-settings-access.md`
- `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`
